verses.1: A Song of degrees. Out of the depths have I cried to thee, O LORD.
verses.2: LORD, hear my voice: let thy ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
verses.3: If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities, O LORD, who shall stand?
verses.4: But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
verses.5: I wait for the LORD, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.
verses.6: My soul waiteth for the LORD more than they that watch for the morning: I say , more than they that watch for the morning.
verses.7: Let Israel hope in the LORD: for with the LORD there is mercy, and with him is plenteous redemption.
verses.8: And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

metadata.source: Webster's Bible (1833) — presented as-is with minor editorial corrections for the ABIDE Bible App.
